channel.title: Delhi news today (नई दिल्ली न्यूज़) | The Hindu
channel.description: Latest Delhi News (नई दिल्ली समाचार): Stay up-to-date with the latest happenings in the ‘City of rallies’. From politics and business to sports, catch all the local news coverage you need about both Old & New Delhi.

image.title: Delhi news today (नई दिल्ली न्यूज़) | The Hindu

item.title: JNUSU results: Left Unity wins three posts, ABVP makes comeback with one post
item.description: ABVP says it won 24 out of 42 councillor posts, claims it is ‘breaching the Left’s red fortress’; will put in effort to work together, says new students’ union president Nitish Kumar

item.title: Delayed response led to widespread destruction: AAP attacks BJP over Rohini fire
₹10-lakh health cover rolled out for Delhiites aged 70 and above; CM, AAP trade barbs over insurance model
Jamia student molested in front of gate; accused arrested
U.P. police summon T.N. trans rights activist Grace Banu over ‘assault’ charges in Lucknow
item.description: Complaint filed by UP Transgender Welfare Board Member Devika Devendra S. Manglamukhi; Ms. Banu says never been to Lucknow, never met Ms. Devika

item.title: Replace all vehicles with EVs in 60 days: Sirsa to Forest Department
Foreign national, who acted in Bollywood films, arrested for peddling drugs
Delhi HC sends 3 CBI officials to agency custody in corruption case
item.description: Court says the situation “shakes the edifice of the machinery” whose primary duty is to bring wrongdoers to justice

item.title: Delhi High Court grants 3-day custody parole to PFI leader OMA Salam
item.description: He wanted to perform certain rituals in Kerala in relation to the death of his daughter last year

item.title: 26/11 Mumbai attack: Delhi court extends Tahawwur Rana’s NIA custody for 12 more days
item.description: Special NIA judge Chander Jit Singh extended Rana's custody on NIA's request after his previous 18-day remand got over

item.title: Supreme Court refuses to entertain fresh plea challenging Waqf (Amendment) Act
item.description: While appointing three lawyers as the nodal counsel, the bench asked the advocates to decide among themselves who was going to argue

item.title: Delhi launches free ₹10 lakh health cover for senior citizens under Vay Vandana scheme
item.description: An unique card under this scheme, will securely store senior citizen’s complete health record, regular health check-up information, and emergency service details

item.title: Delhi CM Rekha Gupta condoles loss of lives in Rohini’s jhuggi cluster, says government helping affected people
item.description: The local MLA and the sub-divisional magistrate immediately reached the spot to coordinate relief efforts, including provision of mobile toilets, medical assistance and food for the affected residents, Ms. Gupta said

item.title: JNUSU results: Left Unity wins three posts, ABVP enters JNUSU after ten years with one
item.description: Vaibhav Meena from ABVP won the Joint Secretary post, making him the first in recent history to have won a central panel post. Meanwhile, ABVP said that it had won 24 councillor posts out of 42.

item.title: DSLSA job fair gives wings to survivors’ cherished dreams
CM vows aid to cowsheds to tackle issue of stray cows
All hands on deck as redeveloped Chandni Chowk rapidly loses lustre
item.description: With the BJP government coming to power in the city, the SRDC has found itself in a tight spot.

item.title: Mohalla buses to see the light of day soon but under a new name
AAP: 2,000 buses removed from DTC fleet; BJP hits back
Two kids die, 800 shanties gutted as massive fire engulfs jhuggi cluster in Delhi’s Rohini
item.description: The fire engulfed over 800 densely packed shanties spread across around five acres. Thick plumes of smoke billowed into the sky as fire fighters battled the flames

item.title: Pahalgam tragedy: Are unresolved questions of Partition reflected in attack, asks Mani Shankar Aiyar
item.description: “There was the partition of 1971, when more than half of Pakistan’s population and a very important part of its territory deliberately moved away from it on the ground that it was not enough to be Muslim and it was also necessary to be Bengali,” Mani Shankar Aiyar said

item.title: MHA deadline to end today: police depts. told to ensure exit of Pakistani nationals
item.description: Systematically identifying Pakistanis in Maharashtra, police will see to it that they leave on time: Fadnavis; many Pak. citizens already on way out, will tally data to ensure compliance: M.P. Police

item.title: Fear in Pakistan refugee camps as MHA visa deadline draws near
item.description: Many Pakistani refugees who possess LTVs and whose applications for citizenship under the Citizenship (Amendment) Act, 2019 are still under consideration said their future in India is uncertain.

item.title: Barbs exchanged over locations of six proposed air quality monitoring units
item.description: Opposition peddling lies, locations were finalised during the term of previous AAP Government, responds Environment Minister Sirsa

item.title: Delhi High Court orders release of Sikh man’s golden kada detained by customs
item.description: The Delhi High Court orders release of Sikh man’s golden kada detained by Customs at IGIA, citing religious significance

item.title: Safdarjung Hospital removes world’s largest adrenal tumour via robotic surgery
item.description: Doctors achieve medical milestone with minimally invasive procedure on 36-year-old woman

item.title: FIITJEE collected ₹206 crore as fees from students of current batches, but did not deliver on services, claims ED
item.description: It said that ₹181.89 crore was collected from 9,823 students for the academic year ending 2025-2026, but the centres in question were closed down

item.title: Six sanitation workers killed, five injured after being hit by vehicle on Delhi Mumbai Expressway in Gurugram
item.description: The accident took place around 10 a.m. when about 11 sanitation workers were cleaning the expressway. Suddenly, a speeding pickup vehicle hit the workers and six of them died on the spot, they said.

item.title: Controversy arises over Gurugram mayor’s husband appointed as advisor
item.description: Congress criticises the move to be against women empowerment

item.title: Man held for speeding away with Delhi constable on car bonnet
Delhi CM allocates ₹125 crore to fire department for purchasing new equipment
Plan in motion to tap Ganga water to clean Yamuna in Delhi
item.description: The Centre is also considering giving U.P. additional funds for its irrigation as compensation for any potential impact.

item.title: Mayoral polls: BJP back at the helm
item.description: Raja Iqbal Singh, Jai Bhagwan Yadav take over as Mayor, Deputy Mayor as BJP assumes control of MCD after little over two years; Singh promises cleaner streets, greener parks, and better air quality; no excuses, new leadership must show results in a month: AAP leader

item.title: DU to conduct “youth run” on One Nation, One Election, teachers question decision
Financial support, women’s security key issues for voters in JNUSU polls
Loneliness epidemic | How new India is making friends
item.description: As more young urban Indians report feeling lonely and friendless, they are joining reading clubs, pickleball groups and more to find companionship

item.title: No Agenda Space: a movement of silent companionship growing across India
item.description: After Bengaluru-based Meghna Chaudhury opened up her home as a ‘third space’ for women and non-binary people this January, the idea quickly spread to other cities

item.title: BJP wins Mayoral poll; Raja Iqbal to be Delhi's new Mayor
item.description: Raja Iqbal was the Leader of the Opposition in the MCD and has previously served as the mayor of North MCD

item.title: Medha Patkar arrest: Delhi court allows release of activist after arrest in defamation case
item.description: Police had arrested the activist after a court had issued a non-bailable warrant against her in a case filed by Delhi Lieutenant-Governor V.K. Saxena.

item.title: Delhi HC orders attachment of Saket Gokhale’s salary in defamation case
item.description: The order had come on former dilpomat Lakshmi Puri’s plea alleging Mr. Gokhale tarnished her goodwill and reputation by making reckless and false allegations about her financial affairs in the context of an apartment that she owned in Geneva.

item.title: Pahalgam attack sparks protest outside Pakistan High Commission in Delhi
item.description: Capital unites in grief and anger, outraged citizens demand action against neighbouring country for ‘backing terrorism’, leading to detentions and solidarity marches

item.title: Promises, disruptions and debate: JNUSU presidential candidates face-off
Migrant families in South Delhi struggle to survive living amid garbage
item.description: Despite having identity cards such as Aadhar, PAN and Ration cards, these settlers are denied beneficiary schemes from the government for being outsiders.

item.title: MCD mayoral polls: will play role of a constructive Opposition, says AAP
Chhattisgarh families thank Kashmiri tourist guide
item.description: Thirty-year-old Nazakat Ahmad Shah was hosting a group of 11, four couples and three children, in the last leg of their Kashmir trip. When the attack took place, he carried two children to safety 14 km away, and also helped the adults.

item.title: An artistic exploration of the seen and unseen
item.description: Art exhibition, Gaze: Exploring Self & Beyond, is an exhibition that captures the essence of seen and unseen human connections

item.title: Phule’s mission is incomplete, says director Ananth Mahadevan on his biopic on social reformer Jyotiba Phule
item.description: Ahead of the release of the biopic, Phule, director Ananth Mahadevan discusses the controversy surrounding the film, the value of creative licence in biopics, and why Pratik Gandhi is the next Sanjeev Kumar

item.title: Railways to run second special train from Jammu's Katra to Delhi on April 24
item.description: It will start from Shri Vaishno Devi Katra, and halt at several stations such as Udhampur, Jammu, Pathankot, Jalandhar, Ambala, Kurukshetra and Panipat before terminating at the New Delhi Railway Station

item.title: Pahalgam attack masterminded by Pak; BJP exploiting tragedy to promote polarisation: Congress
item.description: The meeting started with leaders paying homage to the victims of the attack by observing a moment of silence in their memory

item.title: Delhi High Court asks Swiggy, Zepto to reply to plea for making apps more accessible to visually impaired
item.description: Justice Sachin Datta issued notice to the quick commerce platforms and the Centre on the petition by NGO Mission Accessibility

item.title: FIITJEE closure: ED conducts searches at 8 locations in Delhi NCR
item.description: In January 2025, several FIITJEE centres across the country abruptly closed down without any notice

item.title: ‘End of life’ vehicles to be denied fuel in Delhi from July 1
item.description: Similar restrictions to be imposed in 5 NCR districts, including Gurugram, Ghaziabad; entry of vehicles not registered in the Capital into the city banned from November 1

item.title: Indigenously developed HPV test kits for cervical cancer screening launched in Delhi
item.description: Besides the high incidence of cervical cancer owing to its late diagnosis and with consequent poor survival, 25% of global mortality due to cervical cancer occurs in India, said former head of the department of Obstetrics and Gynaecology at AIIMS, Delhi

item.title: DDA records revenue surplus of ₹1,371 crore in FY 2024-25
Delhi HC declines Ashok Swain’s plea to expunge remarks from order
JNUSU presidential debate held
New electric buses to not carry ads despite suggestion by CAG
Security beefed up in city following Pahalgam terror attack
Pahalgam terror attack: Swiss visa rejection led newly-wed Naval officer to a nightmare honeymoon in Pahalgam
item.description: The couple planned to honeymoon in Switzerland, but changed plans after visa denial, says family; “Pakistan should be taught a lesson... should be ruined,” says relative

item.title: Security beefed up in Delhi following terror attack in Pahalgam
item.description: Delhi Police has ramped up security across the city, focusing particularly on the tourist hotspots and border checkpoints, officials said

item.title: Five years after 2020 Delhi riots, court frames charges of vandalism and arson against 57 persons
item.description: Additional sessions judge (ASJ) Pulastya Pramachala on March 15, had said that the evidence on the record prima facie shows that the accused persons were part of an unlawful assembly, which came into action with the common objective of going on rampage and damage properties.

item.title: Delhi continues to face power cuts amid scorching heat: AAP
AAP accuses Delhi govt. of halting widow pension
Jangpura Madrasi Camp residents protest against shifting to Narela
February 2020 riots: court orders framing of charges against 57
Will focus on cleaning up Capital, says BJP’s Deputy Mayor candidate
item.description: Jai Bhagwan Yadav says after winning mayoral polls, BJP will speed up formation of Standing Committee, resume stalled development works

item.title: JNUSU polls to be held on April 25, EC resumes elections process
Consistency key to Delhiite’s 5th rank in UPSC exam
Saha addresses flood fears over Bangladesh border embankment
item.description: Manik Saha says his government has taken necessary measures in response to the construction of a massive embankment by the Bangladesh government along the border in South Tripura district

item.title: NGT pulls up authorities over management of waste at Bandhwari landfill in Gurugram
Atishi’s security cover to be scaled down to ‘Y’category
item.description: The move comes after Delhi Police’s security unit sought MHA’s guidance on the status of her security cover

item.title: Daughters and sons of Delhi | Keshava Guha on his new novel, The Tiger’s Share
item.description: Guha uses the form of the Victorian novel to write about the people and disputes he witnessed in the capital city

item.title: Campaigning continues in JNU, but election processes remain halted
AAP bows out of MCD race, BJP set to form ‘triple engine’ govt.
‘Wasn’t consulted’: Maharashtra language panel chief on Hindi row
item.description: The committee has already written a letter to Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis advising to revoke the decision

item.title: ‘Sab Yaad Rakha Jayega’: poet Aamir Aziz accuses artist Anita Dube of ‘theft, erasure’ of his work
Key eyewitness in murder shot dead in Shahdara: police reveal conspiracy
Delhi L-G orders magisterial inquiry into Mustafabad building collapse
2020 Delhi riots case: court extends stay on probe against Kapil Mishra till May 7
Delhi CM Rekha Gupta unveils action plan to combat heatwaves
item.description: ‘Heatwaves are no longer just a weather condition but a “deadly crisis”, says Delhi CM Rekha Gupta

item.title: 2024 protest case: Delhi court summons Trinamool leaders Derek O’Brien, Sagarika Ghose, Saket Gokhale, others
item.description: Additional chief judicial magistrate Neha Mittal also summoned TMC leaders Santanu Sen, Dola Sen, Nadimul Haque, Vivek Gupta, Arpita Ghosh, Abir Ranjan Bishwas and Sudip Raha to appear on April 30

item.title: Heavy traffic snarls across Delhi over U.S. Vice President J.D. Vance's visit
item.description: Commuters heading to work in Delhi from adjoining Noida and Ghaziabad in Uttar Pradesh and Gurugram in Haryana found themselves stuck for a long time due to the traffic congestion

item.title: AAP lost faith of people; its councillors unhappy: Delhi CM Rekha Gupta
item.description: Rekha Gupta claimed that the AAP MLAs and councillors are unhappy with their party leadership for adopting "anti-people" policies while being in power

item.title: AAP to boycott MCD mayor elections scheduled for April 25
item.description: She said her party does not believe in the "politics of sabotage and horse-trading"; BJP's current strength in the MCD has increased to 119 following multiple defections from the AAP over the past few months.

item.title: 2020 Delhi riots: Court extends stay on further probe against Kapil Mishra till May 7
item.description: Additional Chief Judicial Magistrate (ACJM) Vaibhav Chaurasia said on April 1 that a cognisable offence was "prima facie" found that needed to be investigated.

item.title: Delhi airport to reopen runway RW 10/28 in May 1st week amid traffic congestion
item.description: Delhi airport suspends critical ILS upgrade, delays runway maintenance, and reduces arrival capacity due to congestion

item.title: Mohalla Clinics open, but rumours of their closure dent footfall
item.description: On condition of anonymity, a doctor at a Mohalla Clinic in south Delhi said low footfall has resulted in a drop in income for the medical staff members.

item.title: Day after building collapse, MCD cracks down on unsafe structures
item.description: Civic body suspends officials for negligence; begins survey to identify, seal buildings in unauthorised colonies; 15 properties flagged; system riddled with corruption: Mayor; Cong. seeks fair compensation for victims, judicial inquiry into the incident

item.title: Blame game emerges in JNU after student union polls postponed over violence
Seelampur teen stabbing case: 2 minors among 8 held
Heat action plan to focus on cooling public spaces
item.description: The plan will include deploying cool roof technology at high footfall areas besides creating ‘cool rooms’ in government hospitals, a senior official told The Hindu.

item.title: Delhi CM flags off GPS-enabled water tankers; exist since 2015, claims AAP
item.description: The Aam Aadmi Party (AAP), however, accused the Bharatiya Janata Party (BJP) government of “spreading misinformation” and “rebranding” initiatives launched by the previous government as new.

item.title: To address demand of loco pilots, Railways fitting toilets in engines
item.description: Waterless urinal facilities provided in 883 locomotives since 2018, wherever feasible, says Ministry; 7,075 locos fitted with ACs; toilets being fitted in all new locos, older ones being retrofitted, says official

item.title: In no mood to be polite: J&K CM Omar Abdullah expresses frustration over flight diversion
item.description: “3 hours in the air after we left Jammu we get diverted to Jaipur & so here I am at 1 in the morning on the steps of the plane getting some fresh air,” CM Abdullah said in a post on X

item.title: Delhi’s illegal borewell boom running on empty
Mustafabad tragedy: BJP slams AAP over illegal constructions
item.description: Kapil Mishra terms collapse a ‘criminal negligence’, while Virendra Sachdeva describes unauthorised buildings as ‘ticking time bombs’

item.title: Delhi police submit report in public property defacement case against Kejriwal, others
item.description: Report filed at Rouse Avenue court; police seek more time to complete the probe; next hearing on May 3

item.title: Mustafabad building collapse: a night of horror, a morning of grief
item.description: Four-storey building collapsed in Mustafabad in the early hours of Saturday

item.title: Delhi-NCR schools flout RTE Act, detain students in Classes 6 and 7
item.description: Department of School Education and Literacy amends rules allowing student detention in Classes 5 and 8 only after re-examination

item.title: In action there is hope for climate change
item.description: Experts get together at a seminar in Delhi to explore how to deal with climate anxiety
